title: wedelia acapulcensis var. acapulcensis
intro: Wedelia acapulcensis var. acapulcensis, also known as Mexican plantain, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Wedelia acapulcensis var. acapulcensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, and are also used in folk remedies to treat a range of health conditions.
